session.thinking:
The user wants me to run the `/erk:objective-update-with-landed-pr` skill with the parameters from the failed command: `--pr 7919 --objective 7823 --branch plnd/07823-plan-im` (the branch name is truncated but that's likely `plnd/07823-plan-im...` something).
Let me invoke the skill with those parameters.
session.user:
# /erk:objective-update-with-landed-pr
Update an objective issue after landing a PR from a plan branch.
## Usage
Run after landing a PR:
```bash /erk:objective-update-with-landed-pr ```
---
## Agent Instructions
> **Design note:** All steps run inline in the caller's context (no subagent delegation). > This is deliberate — Step 3 (prose reconciliation) requires judgment that benefits from > the caller's model quality, and Step 7 (closing prompt) requires direct user interaction.
### Step 1: Fetch All Context
Check `--pr 7919 --objective 7823 --branch plnd/07823-plan-im` for optional overrides:
- `--pr <number>`: PR number that was just landed - `--objective <number>`: Objective issue number to update - `--branch <name>`: Original branch name - `--auto-close`: If set and all nodes are complete, close objective without asking
Run a single command to fetch and parse everything (all options are auto-discovered if omitted):
```bash erk exec objective-fetch-context [--pr <number>] [--objective <number>] [--branch <name>] ```
This returns JSON with:
- `objective`: Issue body, title, labels, URL - `plan`: Plan issue body and title - `pr`: PR title, body, URL - `roadmap.matched_steps`: Node IDs where `plan == #<plan_number>` (deterministic match) - `roadmap.phases`: Serialized roadmap phases - `roadmap.summary`: Node counts (done, pending, etc.) - `roadmap.next_step`: First pending node or null - `roadmap.all_complete`: True if every node is done or skipped
If this returns `success: false`, display the error and stop.
### Step 2: Update Roadmap Nodes
Read `matched_steps` from the context blob. These are the nodes this plan completed — no analysis needed. If `matched_steps` is empty, the plan may not have been linked to specific nodes; fall back to comparing the plan body against the roadmap to identify which nodes were completed.
**CRITICAL: Pass ALL completed nodes as multiple `--node` flags in ONE command. Do NOT run separate commands per node — sequential calls cause race conditions and duplicate API calls.**
Before running update-objective-node, extract the existing plan reference for each completed node from the objective roadmap YAML (available in the context blob). Pass `--plan "#<plan-number>"` to preserve it, or `--plan ""` if the node had no plan.
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-number> --node <node-id-1> --node <node-id-2> ... --pr "#<pr-number>" --plan "#<plan-number>" --status done --include-body ```
Preserve the existing plan reference for each node (available in `roadmap.phases`). The `--include-body` flag returns the fully-mutated body as `updated_body` — use this for prose reconciliation (do NOT re-fetch via `gh issue view`).
### Step 3: Perform Prose Reconciliation
Compare `objective.objective_content` (the prose from the first comment's `objective-body` block) against what the PR actually did. Note: `updated_body` from Step 2 contains only metadata (roadmap); prose lives in `objective_content`.
If `objective_content` is null, skip prose reconciliation entirely (objective has no prose comment).
- Read each Design Decision. Did the PR override or refine any? - Read Implementation Context. Does the architecture description still match? - Read upcoming node descriptions. Did this PR change the landscape? - If nothing is stale, skip prose update entirely.
| Contradiction Type | Example | Section to Update | | --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------- | | **Decision override** | Objective says "Use polling", PR implemented WebSockets | Design Decisions | | **Scope change** | Node says "Add 3 methods", PR only needed 2 | Node description in roadmap | | **Architecture drift** | Context says "config in config.py", PR moved it to settings/ | Implementation Context | | **Constraint invalidation** | Requirement listed is no longer valid | Implementation Context | | **New discovery** | PR revealed a caching bug affecting future nodes | Implementation Context or new Design Decision |
### Step 4: Post Action Comment
Post the action comment via the exec script. Provide structured JSON on stdin:
```bash echo '{"issue_number": <N>, "date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "pr_number": <N>, "phase_step": "X.Y, X.Z", "title": "Brief title", "what_was_done": ["..."], "lessons_learned": ["..."], "roadmap_updates": ["Node X.Y: status -> done"], "body_reconciliation": [{"section": "...", "change": "..."}]}' | erk exec objective-post-action-comment ```
**Inferring content (DO NOT ask the user):**
- **What Was Done:** Infer from PR title, description, and plan body. - **Lessons Learned:** Infer from implementation patterns. If straightforward, note what worked well. - **Body Reconciliation:** Only include if prose sections needed updating. Omit entirely if nothing is stale.
### Step 5: Update Objective Prose (First Comment)
**Only if prose reconciliation found stale sections**, update the objective's first comment (where prose lives). Use `extract_objective_header_comment_id` from the objective body to get the comment ID, then update the comment with reconciled prose wrapped in the `objective-body` metadata block.
To get the comment ID, parse it from `objective.body`'s `objective-header` metadata block (the `objective_comment_id` field).
```bash # Update the first comment (not the issue body) with reconciled prose gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments/{comment_id} -X PATCH -f body="<updated comment body>" ```
If nothing is stale, skip this step entirely. The issue body (`updated_body`) contains only metadata and should NOT be the target of prose updates.
### Step 6: Validate Objective
```bash erk objective check <issue-number> --json-output ```
If checks fail, report failures and attempt to fix. The JSON output now includes `all_complete`.
### Step 7: Check Closing Triggers
Use `all_complete` from the validation output to determine next action.
**If `all_complete` is `true`:**
- **If `--auto-close` was provided:** Post "Action: Objective Complete" comment, close the issue (`gh issue close <issue-number>`), report: "All nodes complete - objective closed automatically" - **Otherwise:** Ask the user directly: ``` All roadmap nodes are complete. Should I close objective #<number> now? - Yes, close with final summary - Not yet, there may be follow-up work - I'll close it manually later ``` If yes: post final comment and close. Otherwise: acknowledge and report complete.
**If `all_complete` is `false`:**
- Report the update is complete. Use `roadmap.next_step` to describe next focus.
---
## Output Format
- **Start:** "Updating objective #<number> after landing PR #<pr-number>" - **After writes:** "Posted action comment and updated objective body for #<number>" - **End:** Either "Objective #<number> closed" or "Objective updated. Next focus: [next action]" - **Always:** Display the objective URL
---
## Error Cases
| Scenario | Action | | ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | | `objective-fetch-context` fails | Display error from JSON and stop | | Issue not found | Report error and exit | | Issue has no `erk-objective` label | Warn user this may not be an objective issue |

</details>
<!-- /erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->


---

## Summary

Improve code-level documentation for backpressure gates

Enhanced docstrings across gate and human-facing functions to clarify the agent backpressure pattern, caller relationships, and schema details. Updated `agent-backpressure-gates.md` to replace a detailed example with a gate inventory table, making it easier for agents to locate gate implementations and their counterparts. All changes are documentation-only with no behavioral modifications.

## Files Changed

<details>
<summary>Files Changed</summary>

### Modified (3 files)
- `docs/learned/architecture/agent-backpressure-gates.md` - Replaced example section with gate inventory table, updated audit timestamp
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/tripwire_candidates.py` - Enhanced docstrings for `validate_candidates_data` and `validate_candidates_json`
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py` - Added gate pattern section header and expanded docstrings for all gate and human-facing functions

</details>

## Key Changes

- Replaced narrative example with gate inventory table in `agent-backpressure-gates.md` linking validation gates to human-facing counterparts and source locations
- Enhanced `validate_plan_title`, `validate_worktree_name`, and `validate_objective_slug` docstrings with agent-facing caller lists and human-facing counterpart relationships
- Clarified pre-gate recovery layer in `validate_candidates_data` docstring to document the two-stage validation approach
- Added header comment in `naming.py` to document the gate/counterpart pattern across the module
- Improved `generate_filename_from_title` and `sanitize_worktree_name` docstrings to explain their role as silent transformations that bypass gates

**Remotely executed:** [Run #22287543551](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22287543551)

---


To checkout this PR in a fresh worktree and environment locally, run:

```
source "$(erk pr checkout 7919 --script)" && erk pr sync --dangerous
```


<details>
<summary>original-plan</summary>

# Plan: Improve Code-Level Documentation for Backpressure Gates

## Context

Objective #7823 is closed with all code landed. The remaining gap (node 3.3) is documenting agent-facing vs human-facing path distinction. Rather than adding a learned doc, improve the code-level documentation directly and have `agent-backpressure-gates.md` point readers to the code.

## Files to Modify

1. **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py`** — Improve module-level and function docstrings for `validate_plan_title`, `validate_worktree_name`, `generate_filename_from_title`, `sanitize_worktree_name`
2. **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/tripwire_candidates.py`** — Improve docstrings for `validate_candidates_data`, `validate_candidates_json`
3. **`docs/learned/architecture/agent-backpressure-gates.md`** — Replace the single example with a brief "see the code" pointer listing the four gate functions and their locations

## Changes

### naming.py

- Add a section comment grouping the gate functions (e.g., `# --- Agent Backpressure Gates ---`)
- Enhance `validate_plan_title` docstring: list the agent-facing callers (`plan_save_to_issue`, `plan_save`, `issue_title_to_filename`) and note the human-facing bypass (`generate_filename_from_title`)
- Enhance `validate_worktree_name` docstring: note it's an internal consistency check (validates system-generated names, not direct agent input), list callers via `prepare_plan_for_worktree` and `setup_impl_from_issue`, note human bypass is `sanitize_worktree_name`
- Enhance `generate_filename_from_title` and `sanitize_worktree_name` docstrings: note these are human-facing silent transformation counterparts to the gates above

### tripwire_candidates.py

- Enhance `validate_candidates_data` docstring: list agent-facing callers (`normalize_tripwire_candidates`, `store_tripwire_candidates`), note the pre-gate recovery layer (`normalize_candidates_data`)

### agent-backpressure-gates.md

- Replace the "Example: Objective Slug Validation" section with a "Gate Inventory" section that simply lists the four gate functions with file paths and a one-line description, directing readers to the code docstrings for details
- Update `last_audited` in frontmatter

## Verification

1. Run `make fast-ci` to ensure no formatting/lint issues


</details>","headRefName":"plnd/O7823-plan-improve-code-le-02-22-2315","title":"Improve code-level documentation for backpressure gates","url":"https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/7919"}

session.tool_result:
{"success": true, "objective": {"number": 7823, "title": "Objective: Apply Agent Back Pressure Gates Across Erk", "body": "
objective-header ```yaml created_at: '2026-02-22T06:01:45.531065+00:00' created_by: schrockn objective_comment_id: 3940710509 slug: agent-backpressure-gates ```
objective-roadmap ```yaml schema_version: '3' nodes: - id: '1.1' description: Add schema validation function that returns ValidTripwireCandidates | InvalidTripwireCandidates with actionable feedback status: in_progress plan: '#7836' pr: null - id: '1.2' description: Wire validation into normalize_tripwire_candidates.py — reject malformed JSON before normalizing status: in_progress plan: '#7836' pr: null - id: '1.3' description: Update agent guidance (skill/command docs) to include the expected schema status: in_progress plan: '#7836' pr: null - id: '2.1' description: Add validate_plan_title() returning ValidPlanTitle | InvalidPlanTitle with minimum content requirements status: done plan: '#7845' pr: '#7845' - id: '2.2' description: Wire validation into agent-facing paths (plan-save, plan-create) upstream of generate_filename_from_title() status: in_progress plan: '#7846' pr: null - id: '2.3' description: Keep generate_filename_from_title() as-is for human-facing paths status: in_progress plan: '#7846' pr: null - id: '3.1' description: Add validate_worktree_name() that checks if a name would survive sanitization without significant transformation status: in_progress plan: '#7889' pr: '#7850' - id: '3.2' description: Wire validation into erk implement and plan-derived worktree creation paths status: done plan: '#7866' pr: '#7866' - id: '3.3' description: Document which paths are agent-facing (gated) vs human-facing (transformed) status: planning plan: null pr: '#7897' ```
", "state": "CLOSED", "labels": ["erk-objective"], "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/issues/7823", "objective_content": "# Objective: Apply Agent Back Pressure Gates Across Erk > Replace silent transformation of agent-generated output with validation gates that reject invalid input and provide actionable feedback for self-correction. ## Goal All agent-facing input paths in erk either (a) validate and reject invalid input with actionable error messages, or (b) are explicitly documented as human-facing (where silent transformation is appropriate UX). No agent output is silently normalized without the agent knowing its input was wrong. ## Design Decisions - **Gates for agents, transforms for humans**: When the producer is an agent, reject invalid input. When the producer is a human, silent transformation is acceptable UX. - **Dual-use functions get an upstream validation layer**: Functions like `sanitize_worktree_name()` serve both humans and agents. Don't replace the sanitizer -- add a validation gate in agent-facing paths that calls the validator before the sanitizer. - **Follow the ValidX | InvalidX pattern**: Use discriminated unions (not `ErrorType | None`) per the lesson from `validate_objective_slug()`. - **Actionable feedback is mandatory**: Every rejection must include the expected pattern, actual value, and valid/invalid examples so the agent can self-correct. ## Roadmap ### Phase 1: Tripwire Candidate Schema Gate (1 PR) Replace silent JSON schema normalization in normalize_tripwire_candidates with a validation gate. This is the highest-value target because it exclusively processes agent output and the normalization masks schema drift. | Node | Description | Status | Plan | PR | |------|-------------|--------|------|----| | 1.1 | Add schema validation function that returns ValidTripwireCandidates | in-progress | #7836 | - | - | | 1.2 | Wire validation into normalize_tripwire_candidates.py -- reject malformed JSON before normalizing | in-progress | #7836 | - | | 1.3 | Update agent guidance (skill/command docs) to include the expected schema | in-progress | #7836 | - | **Test:** Agent-produced JSON with wrong root key or missing fields is rejected with structured error showing expected schema, not silently remapped ### Phase 2: Plan Title Validation Gate (1 PR) Add validation for agent-provided plan titles in generate_filename_from_title() path. Currently falls back to plan.md silently when title is empty or all special chars. | Node | Description | Status | Plan | PR | |------|-------------|--------|------|----| | 2.1 | Add validate_plan_title() returning ValidPlanTitle | done | #7845 | #7845 | | 2.2 | Wire validation into agent-facing paths (plan-save, plan-create) upstream of generate_filename_from_title() | in-progress | #7846 | - | | 2.3 | Keep generate_filename_from_title() as-is for human-facing paths | in-progress | #7846 | - | **Test:** Agent providing an empty or emoji-only title gets a rejection with rules and examples instead of a silent fallback to plan.md ### Phase 3: Worktree Name Validation Layer (1 PR) Add upstream validation in agent-facing worktree creation paths. The sanitize_worktree_name() function stays for human UX, but agent paths validate first. | Node | Description | Status | Plan | PR | |------|-------------|--------|------|----| | 3.1 | Add validate_worktree_name() that checks if a name would survive sanitization without significant transformation | in-progress | #7889 | #7850 | | 3.2 | Wire validation into erk implement and plan-derived worktree creation paths | done | #7866 | #7866 | | 3.3 | Document which paths are agent-facing (gated) vs human-facing (transformed) | planning | - | #7897 | **Test:** Agent-provided worktree name that would be significantly truncated or transformed is rejected with actionable feedback showing naming rules
objective-roadmap ```yaml schema_version: '3' nodes: - id: '1.1' description: Add schema validation function that returns ValidTripwireCandidates | InvalidTripwireCandidates with actionable feedback status: pending plan: null pr: null - id: '1.2' description: Wire validation into normalize_tripwire_candidates.py — reject malformed JSON before normalizing status: pending plan: null pr: null - id: '1.3' description: Update agent guidance (skill/command docs) to include the expected schema status: pending plan: null pr: null - id: '2.1' description: Add validate_plan_title() returning ValidPlanTitle | InvalidPlanTitle with minimum content requirements status: pending plan: null pr: null - id: '2.2' description: Wire validation into agent-facing paths (plan-save, plan-create) upstream of generate_filename_from_title() status: pending plan: null pr: null - id: '2.3' description: Keep generate_filename_from_title() as-is for human-facing paths status: pending plan: null pr: null - id: '3.1' description: Add validate_worktree_name() that checks if a name would survive sanitization without significant transformation status: pending plan: null pr: null - id: '3.2' description: Wire validation into erk implement and plan-derived worktree creation paths status: pending plan: null pr: null - id: '3.3' description: Document which paths are agent-facing (gated) vs human-facing (transformed) status: pending plan: null pr: null ```"}, "plan": {"number": "7919", "title": "Improve code-level documentation for backpressure gates", "body": "# Plan: Improve Code-Level Documentation for Backpressure Gates ## Context Objective #7823 is closed with all code landed. The remaining gap (node 3.3) is documenting agent-facing vs human-facing path distinction. Rather than adding a learned doc, improve the code-level documentation directly and have `agent-backpressure-gates.md` point readers to the code. ## Files to Modify 1. **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py`** — Improve module-level and function docstrings for `validate_plan_title`, `validate_worktree_name`, `generate_filename_from_title`, `sanitize_worktree_name` 2. **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/tripwire_candidates.py`** — Improve docstrings for `validate_candidates_data`, `validate_candidates_json` 3. **`docs/learned/architecture/agent-backpressure-gates.md`** — Replace the single example with a brief "see the code" pointer listing the four gate functions and their locations ## Changes ### naming.py - Add a section comment grouping the gate functions (e.g., `# --- Agent Backpressure Gates ---`) - Enhance `validate_plan_title` docstring: list the agent-facing callers (`plan_save_to_issue`, `plan_save`, `issue_title_to_filename`) and note the human-facing bypass (`generate_filename_from_title`) - Enhance `validate_worktree_name` docstring: note it's an internal consistency check (validates system-generated names, not direct agent input), list callers via `prepare_plan_for_worktree` and `setup_impl_from_issue`, note human bypass is `sanitize_worktree_name` - Enhance `generate_filename_from_title` and `sanitize_worktree_name` docstrings: note these are human-facing silent transformation counterparts to the gates above ### tripwire_candidates.py - Enhance `validate_candidates_data` docstring: list agent-facing callers (`normalize_tripwire_candidates`, `store_tripwire_candidates`), note the pre-gate recovery layer (`normalize_candidates_data`) ### agent-backpressure-gates.md - Replace the "Example: Objective Slug Validation" section with a "Gate Inventory" section that simply lists the four gate functions with file paths and a one-line description, directing readers to the code docstrings for details - Update `last_audited` in frontmatter ## Verification 1. Run `make fast-ci` to ensure no formatting/lint issues "}, "pr": {"number": 7919, "title": "Improve code-level documentation for backpressure gates", "body": "
plan-header ```yaml schema_version: '2' created_at: '2026-02-22T23:15:08.911178+00:00' created_by: schrockn plan_comment_id: null last_dispatched_run_id: '22287543551' last_dispatched_node_id: WFR_kwLOPxC3hc8AAAAFMHDs_w last_dispatched_at: '2026-02-22T23:17:31.560632+00:00' last_local_impl_at: null last_local_impl_event: null last_local_impl_session: null last_local_impl_user: null last_remote_impl_at: '2026-02-22T23:22:54.918004+00:00' last_remote_impl_run_id: null last_remote_impl_session_id: null branch_name: plnd/O7823-plan-improve-code-le-02-22-2315 objective_issue: 7823 created_from_session: dee89d6c-22cf-4099-931e-3b50225d748a lifecycle_stage: implemented last_session_branch: async-learn/7919 last_session_id: 0821153b-43b3-45bb-a720-fd57d97c7fd8 last_session_at: '2026-02-22T23:23:55.583421+00:00' last_session_source: remote worktree_name: erk ```
--- ## Summary Improve code-level documentation for backpressure gates Enhanced docstrings across gate and human-facing functions to clarify the agent backpressure pattern, caller relationships, and schema details. Updated `agent-backpressure-gates.md` to replace a detailed example with a gate inventory table, making it easier for agents to locate gate implementations and their counterparts. All changes are documentation-only with no behavioral modifications. ## Files Changed
Files Changed ### Modified (3 files) - `docs/learned/architecture/agent-backpressure-gates.md` - Replaced example section with gate inventory table, updated audit timestamp - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/tripwire_candidates.py` - Enhanced docstrings for `validate_candidates_data` and `validate_candidates_json` - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py` - Added gate pattern section header and expanded docstrings for all gate and human-facing functions
## Key Changes - Replaced narrative example with gate inventory table in `agent-backpressure-gates.md` linking validation gates to human-facing counterparts and source locations - Enhanced `validate_plan_title`, `validate_worktree_name`, and `validate_objective_slug` docstrings with agent-facing caller lists and human-facing counterpart relationships - Clarified pre-gate recovery layer in `validate_candidates_data` docstring to document the two-stage validation approach - Added header comment in `naming.py` to document the gate/counterpart pattern across the module - Improved `generate_filename_from_title` and `sanitize_worktree_name` docstrings to explain their role as silent transformations that bypass gates **Remotely executed:** [Run #22287543551](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22287543551) --- To checkout this PR in a fresh worktree and environment locally, run: ``` source "$(erk pr checkout 7919 --script)" && erk pr sync --dangerous ```
original-plan # Plan: Improve Code-Level Documentation for Backpressure Gates ## Context Objective #7823 is closed with all code landed. The remaining gap (node 3.3) is documenting agent-facing vs human-facing path distinction. Rather than adding a learned doc, improve the code-level documentation directly and have `agent-backpressure-gates.md` point readers to the code. ## Files to Modify 1. **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py`** — Improve module-level and function docstrings for `validate_plan_title`, `validate_worktree_name`, `generate_filename_from_title`, `sanitize_worktree_name` 2. **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/tripwire_candidates.py`** — Improve docstrings for `validate_candidates_data`, `validate_candidates_json` 3. **`docs/learned/architecture/agent-backpressure-gates.md`** — Replace the single example with a brief "see the code" pointer listing the four gate functions and their locations ## Changes ### naming.py - Add a section comment grouping the gate functions (e.g., `# --- Agent Backpressure Gates ---`) - Enhance `validate_plan_title` docstring: list the agent-facing callers (`plan_save_to_issue`, `plan_save`, `issue_title_to_filename`) and note the human-facing bypass (`generate_filename_from_title`) - Enhance `validate_worktree_name` docstring: note it's an internal consistency check (validates system-generated names, not direct agent input), list callers via `prepare_plan_for_worktree` and `setup_impl_from_issue`, note human bypass is `sanitize_worktree_name` - Enhance `generate_filename_from_title` and `sanitize_worktree_name` docstrings: note these are human-facing silent transformation counterparts to the gates above ### tripwire_candidates.py - Enhance `validate_candidates_data` docstring: list agent-facing callers (`normalize_tripwire_candidates`, `store_tripwire_candidates`), note the pre-gate recovery layer (`normalize_candidates_data`) ### agent-backpressure-gates.md - Replace the "Example: Objective Slug Validation" section with a "Gate Inventory" section that simply lists the four gate functions with file paths and a one-line description, directing readers to the code docstrings for details - Update `last_audited` in frontmatter ## Verification 1. Run `make fast-ci` to ensure no formatting/lint issues

### Step 3: Prose Reconciliation
The `objective_content` contains: - The goal text - still accurate - Design Decisions - still accurate (gates for agents, transforms for humans - this is what the PR documented!) - Roadmap table - this is auto-generated and shouldn't be manually edited
Looking at the prose sections: - "Gates for agents, transforms for humans" - this is exactly what the PR documented via code docstrings and the gate inventory table. The prose still accurately describes the pattern. - "Dual-use functions get an upstream validation layer" - still accurate
- "Follow the ValidX | InvalidX pattern" - still accurate - "Actionable feedback is mandatory" - still accurate
The PR was purely documentation - it didn't change any behavior or architectural decisions. The prose sections are still accurate, so no updates needed.
